primary_sources.finding: Strong If-Match preconditions prevent state-changing methods from applying after the selected representation changes, addressing lost updates.
Canonical JSON creates an invariant hashable representation for repeatable digest comparison.

finding: A process-local duplicate map cannot prove cross-run uniqueness. Terminal peer-execution records need a durable compare-and-set boundary keyed by trigger ID, with canonical digest-derived validators, idempotent same-write acceptance, conflict rejection, and bounded contention failure.
design_implication: Use createDurablePeerTerminalJournal as the appendEvent persistence boundary for peer-execution-runtime. Backends must implement read plus atomic compareAndSet semantics equivalent to a strong-validator precondition; a read-then-write sequence without atomic enforcement is insufficient.

verification.method: node --test against byte-identical candidate source before commit

uncertainty: No production durable backend is wired. Correctness still depends on compareAndSet being truly atomic and strongly consistent for the selected key.
falsification_route: Run two independent processes against the production backend using the same trigger ID but different terminal events. Exactly one write may be recorded; the other must receive terminal-conflict or precondition exhaustion, and a fresh read must expose only one terminal record.
